Home»Sport»Paris Olympics 2024»Tatjana on her golden performance: ‘For my country’

Hear what South Africa’s golden girl Tatjana Smith had to say about her performance!

Tatjana Smith won the gold medal in the women’s 100m breaststroke at the Olympics after a surging second half swim in Paris on Monday night.

In a thrilling 100m breaststroke final, China’s Quinting Tang led the race for about 95m, with Ireland’s Mona McSharry and Italy’s Benedetto Pilato also in podium positions down the home stretch.

Tatjana, who was in fourth place when they turned at the wall, but she fought her way back into contention in the final 50m of the swim, as she first moved past Pilato and McSharry before beating Tang as the first swimmer totouch the wall for gold.

Her super victory bringsSouth Africa’s medal tally to three, after the Blitzboks won bronze in the rugby andmountain biker Alan Hatherlyalso secured bronze in the men’s cross-country race.

This means Team SA has already equalled its medal tally of the 2020 Olympics, when they had scooped a gold and two silver medals.

““I didn’t want to focus on anyone,” Tatjana said after her golden swim. “I knew the only way I could be the best version of myself was to focus on my own race. I’m just so grateful it was my hands that touched first.”

“In the final, if you have a lane, you have a chance. I think we were all fighting,” an emotional Tatjana  added.

“The emotions were really … it took a village to get me where I am today. I know they would have been proud, even if I didn’t get a medal, but it was really for them,” Smith said.

“It was for everyone that has been with me, who believed in me and for my country as well. Our hashtag is ‘for my country …’ it really is, just to inspire someone at home and to know that they don’t have to disqualify themselves and potentially be an Olympic champion,” Tatjana further explained.

“But the work is not done, I still have the 200 to go!”
